expectations.text: ponder.config.ts reads deployedContracts from SE-2 nextjs package
expectations.evidence: ponder.config.ts imports ABI from a local ./abis/YourContract file and uses a hardcoded chainId 31337 with an env var for the address. It does NOT import or read deployedContracts from the SE-2 nextjs package (packages/nextjs/contracts/deployedContracts.ts).

expectations.text: ponder.config.ts reads scaffoldConfig for network detection
expectations.evidence: ponder.config.ts hardcodes the localhost chain with id 31337 and an env-var RPC URL. There is no import of scaffoldConfig from packages/nextjs/scaffold.config.ts for network detection.

expectations.text: Package named @se-2/ponder following SE-2 workspace convention
expectations.evidence: packages/ponder/package.json has "name": "@se-2/ponder", which follows the SE-2 workspace naming convention (@se-2/<package>).

expectations.text: Uses Ponder virtual module imports (ponder:registry, ponder:schema, ponder:api)
expectations.evidence: src/index.ts imports from 'ponder:registry' and 'ponder:schema'. src/api/index.ts imports from 'ponder:api' and 'ponder:schema'. All three virtual module imports are used.

expectations.text: Schema uses onchainTable (not older createSchema API)
expectations.evidence: ponder.schema.ts uses: import { index, onchainTable } from 'ponder'; and exports greetingChange = onchainTable('greeting_change', ...). No createSchema usage.
src/index.ts uses: ponder.on('YourContract:GreetingChange', async ({ event, context }) => { ... }), which follows the ContractName:EventName format.
src/index.ts uses: await context.db.insert(greetingChange).values({ id: ..., greetingSetter, newGreeting, premium, value, blockNumber, timestamp, transactionHash }).

expectations.text: Hono-based API setup for GraphQL (not old express-style)
expectations.evidence: src/api/index.ts creates a Hono app: const app = new Hono(); with app.use('/', graphql({ db, schema })); and exports default app. Uses hono package which is also listed as a dependency.

expectations.text: Root package.json has ponder proxy scripts
expectations.evidence: Root package.json includes: "ponder:dev": "yarn workspace @se-2/ponder dev", "ponder:start": "yarn workspace @se-2/ponder start", "ponder:codegen": "yarn workspace @se-2/ponder codegen".
No ponder-env.d.ts file exists in the outputs directory. The tsconfig.json references 'ponder-env.d.ts' in its include array, but the actual file was not created.

suggestions: Import deployedContracts from the SE-2 nextjs package to dynamically read contract ABIs and addresses instead of hardcoding them or using separate ABI files.
Import scaffoldConfig from packages/nextjs/scaffold.config.ts to detect the target network dynamically instead of hardcoding localhost/31337.

Create a ponder-env.d.ts type declaration file that provides type definitions for Ponder virtual module imports (ponder:registry, ponder:schema, ponder:api). This is standard in Ponder projects and was referenced in tsconfig.json but never created.
eval_feedback.overall: The implementation covers core Ponder functionality well (7/10): correct use of onchainTable, virtual module imports, Hono-based API, ContractName:EventName handler format, and context.db.insert pattern. However, it fails on SE-2 integration points — it doesn't read from deployedContracts or scaffoldConfig, choosing instead to hardcode values and use environment variables. The missing ponder-env.d.ts file is a minor but important omission for TypeScript support.
